name: 武汉
description: Wuhan, the capital of Hubei Province, is a dynamic metropolis where ancient culture meets modern innovation. Known as the 'River City' for its unique location at the confluence of the Yangtze and Han Rivers, Wuhan offers stunning natural scenery, rich revolutionary history, and one of China's most vibrant food cultures. The city is famous for its cherry blossoms at Wuhan University, the iconic Yellow Crane Tower, and the engineering marvel of the Yangtze River Bridge.
highlights: Yellow Crane Tower - Iconic city symbol
Yangtze River Bridge - First rail-road bridge
East Lake - Largest city lake in China
Hubei Provincial Museum - Ancient Chu culture

climate.tips: Winters can be cold and damp, summers hot and humid. Spring (March-May) is the best time for cherry blossoms at Wuhan University. Autumn offers pleasant weather. Summer brings frequent rainstorms. Pack layers for variable temperatures.

attractions.name: 黄鹤楼
attractions.description: One of the most famous towers in China, dating back 1700 years. Rebuilt multiple times, the current structure is a 5-story tower offering panoramic views of the Yangtze River and the city.
attractions.address: 1蛇山黄鹤楼公园, 武昌区
attractions.tips: Visit at sunset for best views. The tower is beautifully illuminated at night. Combine with a walk along the Yangtze River embankment nearby.

attractions.name: 武汉大学
attractions.description: One of China's most beautiful universities, famous for its cherry blossoms, early 20th-century architecture, and stunning lakeside location. Known as the 'Most Beautiful University in China'.
attractions.openingHours: Open campus,樱花 season typically March-April
attractions.tips: Best time for cherry blossoms is late March. Arrive early morning on weekends to avoid crowds. Bring valid ID for campus entry during peak season.

attractions.name: 武汉长江大桥
attractions.description: The first self-designed and self-constructed bridge over the Yangtze River, built in 1957. A dual-purpose railway-highway bridge that became a symbol of Chinese engineering prowess.
attractions.tips: Walk across for the best experience. Visit at night to see the bridge illuminated. The viewing platform on the railway level offers unique perspectives.

attractions.name: 东湖
attractions.description: The largest urban lake in China, covering 33 square kilometers. A beautiful natural oasis with gardens, cycling paths, and cultural attractions around its shores.
attractions.tips: Rent a bicycle to explore the extensive lake area. The Cherry Blossom Park has over 10,000 cherry trees. Visit in early morning for tai chi practitioners and peaceful scenery.

attractions.name: 湖北省博物馆
attractions.description: One of China's most important provincial museums, featuring artifacts from the ancient Chu state civilization including the famous Sword of King Goujian and the Zenghouyi Bell Set.
attractions.tips: Book tickets online in advance. Audio guides available. The bell performance is a must-see. Allocate extra time for the extensive collections.

attractions.name: 归元寺
attractions.description: A famous Buddhist temple built during the Qing dynasty, known for its 500 Arhat statues with unique expressions and its peaceful atmosphere away from the city center.
attractions.tips: One of the few temples where photos of deities are allowed. The 500 Arhat statues each have unique faces and expressions. A good place for understanding Buddhist art.

attractions.name: 晴川阁
attractions.description: A historic pavilion on the banks of the Yangtze River, offering views of the Yellow Crane Tower across the water. Built in the Ming dynasty and rebuilt multiple times.
attractions.tips: Best visited in combination with the nearby Yellow Crane Tower. The riverside walkway offers pleasant evening strolls. Beautiful during the plum blossom season.

attractions.name: 木兰山
attractions.description: A sacred mountain in Huangpi District, known for its beautiful natural scenery and the legendary Mulan culture. Features ancient temples and stunning rock formations.
attractions.tips: Arrive early to avoid crowds. Wear comfortable hiking shoes. Combine with nearby Mulan Lake for a full day trip. The mountain is considered sacred so maintain respectful behavior.

attractions.name: 汉口江滩
attractions.description: A beautiful riverside area along the Yangtze River in the historic Hankou district. Features the famous ' Jiangtan' or river beach and stunning city skyline views.
attractions.address: 汉口江滩公园, 江岸区
attractions.tips: Visit at sunset and stay for the nightly light show. The Bund area nearby features colonial-era buildings. Good for evening walks and photography.

attractions.name: 江汉路
attractions.description: The most famous shopping street in Wuhan, featuring a mix of modern malls and historic colonial-era buildings. A vibrant pedestrian area perfect for shopping and people-watching.
attractions.tips: Best visited in the evening when the buildings are illuminated. Try local snacks from street vendors. Connects to the Bund area for a full walking tour.

attractions.name: 武汉天地
attractions.description: A trendy lifestyle district in the Hankou area, combining historic industrial architecture with modern entertainment, dining, and cultural spaces.
attractions.address: 芦沟桥路68号, 江岸区
attractions.tips: Great for evening dining and nightlife. Hosts regular cultural events and markets. The former warehouse architecture is impressive. Popular with young locals.

attractions.name: 光谷步行街
attractions.description: A massive pedestrian shopping and entertainment district in the heart of Wuhan's tech hub, featuring international brands, restaurants, and attractions.
attractions.tips: The Spanish Street and Italian Street offer great photo opportunities. The musical fountain show is spectacular. Connected to Wudang Mountain via metro.

attractions.name: 长春观
attractions.description: One of the three famous Taoist temples in China, located in the heart of the city. Features well-preserved Ming and Qing dynasty architecture.
attractions.tips: Less crowded than other temples. Good for understanding Taoist culture. Located near the Yangtze River Bridge. The temple offers Taoist tea ceremonies.

attractions.name: 磨山风景区
attractions.description: Part of East Lake, Mopanshan features the famous楚城 (Chu City) theme park and beautiful gardens. Known for the massive 朱碑亭 (Zhu Bei Pavilion).
attractions.address: 东湖生态旅游风景区磨山
attractions.tips: Take the cable car to the top for panoramic East Lake views. The Chu City architecture is impressive. Best during spring for flowers. Combine with other East Lake attractions.

attractions.name: 昙华林
attractions.description: A historic cultural district with over 600 years of history, featuring traditional architecture, art galleries, cafes, and a blend of old and new Wuhan.
attractions.tips: Best explored on foot to appreciate the alleyways. Visit in the late afternoon and stay for dinner. Many buildings have interesting historical stories. Great for souvenirs and local crafts.

attractions.name: 中山公园
attractions.description: A large urban park in the center of Hankou, featuring lakes, gardens, amusement rides, and the historic Zhongshan Tower. A favorite local recreation area.
attractions.address: 解放大道696号, 江汉区
attractions.tips: Join the morning tai chi groups for a local experience. The park transforms at night with illuminations. Good for families with children. Near the Wuhan International Convention Center.

attractions.name: 宝通寺
attractions.description: The oldest temple in Wuhan, dating back 1600 years. Located at the foot of the iconic Machiu mountain with beautiful ancient architecture and Buddhist artifacts.
attractions.tips: The temple is at the base of Machiu (Snake Hill). Climb the hill for city views. A working temple so maintain appropriate behavior. The vegetarian restaurant is excellent.

attractions.name: 黎黄陂路
attractions.description: A historic street in the Hankou concession area featuring well-preserved colonial architecture, teahouses, cafes, and museums documenting Wuhan's treaty port history.
attractions.tips: Best explored on foot. The street captures Wuhan's international history. Try the traditional snacks from local vendors. Perfect for photography enthusiasts.

attractions.name: 武汉昆虫王国
attractions.description: An educational theme park featuring thousands of insect species, butterfly gardens, and interactive exhibits about the insect world.
attractions.tips: Great for families with children. Best visited in spring and summer for active insects. Wear comfortable walking shoes. Combine with nearby Mulan Mountain.

attractions.name: 楚河汉街
attractions.description: A premium shopping and entertainment district featuring international brands, restaurants, and stunning modern architecture along a man-made canal.
attractions.tips: Beautiful at night when illuminated. Take a boat tour on the canal for a different perspective. The mix of shopping and dining is excellent. Connected to East Lake.

attractions.name: 武汉月湖风景区
attractions.description: A serene urban lake with surrounding parks, gardens, and the famous Moon Lake Moon Bridge. Popular for morning exercise and evening walks.
attractions.tips: Best visited at sunset. The Moon Bridge is beautiful for photos. Combine with a visit to Guiyuan Temple nearby.

attractions.name: 武汉海昌极地海洋世界
attractions.description: A large marine theme park with polar animals, dolphin shows, and underwater tunnels. Perfect for families with children.
attractions.tips: Arrive early to catch all shows. Weekends are crowded. Book tickets online for discounts. Combine with nearby Wuhan Museum.

attractions.name: 武汉博物馆
attractions.description: The largest museum in central China, showcasing Wuhan's history from ancient times to modern era with extensive collections of artifacts and interactive exhibits.
attractions.tips: Free audio guides available. Allocate at least 2 hours. The museum shop has good souvenirs. Near the Wuhan Sea World.

attractions.name: 武汉植物园
attractions.description: One of the three major botanical gardens in China, featuring diverse plant collections, themed gardens, and research facilities on the shores of East Lake.
attractions.tips: Best in spring and summer for flowers. The tropical greenhouse is air-conditioned. Bring sunscreen and water. Great for nature photography.

attractions.name: 武汉革命博物馆
attractions.description: Commemorating Wuhan's revolutionary history with exhibits on the 1911 Xinhai Revolution and the Chinese Communist Party's early activities.
attractions.tips: Near Yellow Crane Tower. Combine with a visit to understand Wuhan's revolutionary significance. English descriptions are limited.

attractions.name: 胜天农庄
attractions.description: A scenic rural retreat near Wuhan with agricultural experiences, recreational activities, and beautiful countryside scenery.
attractions.tips: Good for families. Book accommodation in advance for overnight stays. Spring and autumn are best seasons. Authentic rural experience.

attractions.name: 汉阳陵永陵
attractions.nameEn: Tomb of Emperor Wu of Han
attractions.description: The tomb of Emperor Liu Qi of the Western Han dynasty, featuring archaeological excavations and exhibits of ancient burial customs.
attractions.tips: Less crowded than other sites. Combine with nearby Guiyuan Temple. Good for history enthusiasts.

attractions.name: 武汉欢乐谷
attractions.description: A large amusement park with thrilling rides, live shows, and family-friendly attractions. One of Central China's premier entertainment destinations.
attractions.tips: Buy tickets online in advance. Height restrictions on some rides. Wear comfortable clothes and shoes. Summer water park is popular.

attractions.name: 武汉芙蓉谷漂流
attractions.description: An exciting rafting experience through scenic mountain valleys near Wuhan. Popular summer activity with beautiful natural scenery.
attractions.tips: Book through travel agencies. Wear water shoes and bring change of clothes. Not suitable for young children or elderly. Best in summer.

attractions.name: 黄鹤楼
attractions.description: 中国四大名楼之一，有天下江山第一楼之称。
attractions.tips: 登楼看长江。

attractions.name: 湖北省博物馆
attractions.description: 馆藏文物24万件，以曾侯乙编钟最著名。
attractions.tips: 必看编钟。

attractions.name: 武汉大学
attractions.description: 中国最美丽的大学之一，春季樱花闻名全国。
attractions.tips: 3月樱花最美。

attractions.name: 户部巷
attractions.description: 武汉最著名的小吃街，有热干面等武汉小吃。
attractions.tips: 品尝武汉小吃。

attractions.name: 武汉长江大桥
attractions.description: 中国第一座长江大桥，有万里长江第一桥之称。
attractions.tips: 了解中国桥梁史。

attractions.name: 武汉美食
attractions.description: 武汉有热干面和各种小吃。
attractions.tips: 品尝武汉美食。

attractions.name: 武汉夜景
attractions.description: 长江两岸的夜景灯光秀。
attractions.tips: 夏季夜间最美。

attractions.name: 武汉植物园
attractions.description: 华中地区最大的植物园。
attractions.tips: 休闲好去处。

attractions.name: 武汉动物园
attractions.description: 武汉最大的动物园。
attractions.tips: 适合亲子游。

attractions.name: 武汉科技馆
attractions.description: 华中地区最大的科技馆。
attractions.tips: 适合亲子游。

attractions.name: 木兰天池
attractions.description: 武汉北部的风景区，有天池和瀑布。
attractions.tips: 夏季避暑。

attractions.name: 武汉美术馆
attractions.description: 展示中外美术作品。
attractions.tips: 了解艺术文化。

attractions.name: 华中科技大学
attractions.description: 中国最著名的理工科大学之一。
attractions.tips: 了解武汉教育。

attractions.name: 武昌江滩
attractions.description: 长江边的休闲区。
attractions.tips: 傍晚散步。

attractions.name: 蔡林记
attractions.description: 武汉最有名的热干面店。
attractions.tips: 品尝正宗热干面。

attractions.name: 武汉地铁
attractions.description: 中国重要的城市轨道交通系统。
attractions.tips: 体验武汉交通。

attractions.name: 解放公园
attractions.description: 武汉最大的公园之一。
attractions.tips: 休闲好去处。

attractions.name: 武汉长江二桥
attractions.description: 横跨长江的第二座大桥。
attractions.tips: 看长江风光。

attractions.name: 江汉路
attractions.description: 武汉最繁华的商业步行街。
attractions.tips: 购物好去处。

attractions.name: 汉口老租界
attractions.description: 保留了大量欧式建筑的历史街区。
attractions.tips: 了解武汉历史。

attractions.name: 中山公园
attractions.description: 武汉最大的公园之一。
attractions.tips: 休闲好去处。

hotels.bookingTips: Request river-view rooms for best scenery. Weekend packages often include breakfast.

hotels.name: 武汉中南花园酒店

hotels.name: 武汉晴川假日酒店

hotels.name: 武汉江城明珠豪生大酒店

hotels.name: 武汉纽宾凯徐东国际酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Good for business travelers. Weekend rates often lower. Near the Yangtze River Bridge.

arrival.from: Major international airports via Beijing, Shanghai, Guangzhou
arrival.duration: Varies by origin (2-3 hours from major Chinese cities)
arrival.frequency: Multiple daily flights from all major Chinese cities
arrival.tips: Wuhan has direct flights to many domestic cities. Airport is 26km from city center. Metro Line 2 connects to city center (35¥, 50 minutes).

arrival.to: Wuhan Railway Station / Wuhan Station / Wuchang Station
arrival.tips: Wuhan is a major railway hub with excellent high-speed rail connections. Wuhan Station is for high-speed trains. Wuchang Station has more routes.

metro: Metro maps available in English via app
Line 2 (blue): Major north-south line through city center
Line 4 (orange): Connects railway stations and tourist areas
Line 6 (purple): Scenic route along Yangtze River
Airport Express: Line 2 to Tianhe Airport (35¥)
First train: 06:00, Last train: 23:00 (varies by line)
Women-only cars available on Line 2 during peak hours
bus: Extensive network covering areas not served by metro
Single ride: 2¥ (flat fee for regular buses)
Night buses (通宵车): Available on major routes
Download Wuhan Transport app for real-time bus info
Route numbers: 1-99 for main lines, 100+ for secondary
Tips: Have exact change ready, or use Wuhan Tong card
taxi: Didi app highly recommended (English interface available)
Long distance trips: 2¥ per km after 10km
Airport to city center: 100-150¥ by taxi
Tip: Taxis are plentiful and affordable in Wuhan
bike: Mobike, Hello bikes, and Meituan bikes widely available
Bike lanes on most major roads
Designated parking zones required for shared bikes
Caution: Traffic can be chaotic, stay alert
Great for short trips around the university area
walking: Central Wuhan is walkable between attractions
Use underground passages at major intersections
Walk on right side of sidewalks
Cross at designated crosswalks - jaywalking fines exist
Tanhualin and Jianghan Road areas best explored on foot
rental: Electric scooters: Require helmet and license by law
Car rental: International license accepted with translation
Driver with car: Popular option for day trips to nearby attractions

payment.description: Most widely accepted mobile payment in Wuhan
howToUse: Link international credit card (Visa/Mastercard accepted at many merchants)
QR code payment at most merchants
tips: Most vendors in Wuhan use Alipay exclusively
Download before arrival as app stores may not have it

payment.description: Second most popular payment method, essential for social interactions
howToUse: Link international card through WeChat Pay
tips: Many local services prefer WeChat for authentication

payment.description: Still accepted everywhere, essential for markets and street vendors
howToUse: ATMs widely available (may charge fees for foreign cards)
tips: Keep small bills for tips and small purchases
Bank of China and ICBC ATMs accept foreign cards

payment.description: Accepted at hotels, large restaurants, and international chains
howToUse: Visa and Mastercard widely accepted at upscale venues
tips: Call your bank to enable international transactions
Some places add foreign transaction fees

payment.description: Stored-value card for metro and bus rides
howToUse: Top up at stations or convenience stores
Tap to pay on metro and buses

payment.description: China's domestic card network, widely accepted
howToUse: ATMs accepting UnionPay are widely available
Many POS terminals accept UnionPay cards
tips: Works at most ATMs in China
Some international cards may work as UnionPay

culturalTips.title: Dialect Note
culturalTips.content: Wuhan dialect (武汉话) is known for its direct and forceful tone. It's part of the Southwestern Mandarin group. locals appreciate when visitors try simple phrases like '吃了没' (have you eaten).

culturalTips.title: Wuhan Food Culture
culturalTips.content: Wuhan is famous for过早 (guozao) - breakfast culture. locals take breakfast very seriously with numerous street food options. The phrase '过早了没' is a common morning greeting.

culturalTips.title: Hot Dry Noodles
culturalTips.content: 热干面 (hot dry noodles) is Wuhan's iconic dish. The芝麻酱 (sesame paste) coating can be an acquired taste - start with a small portion to try.

culturalTips.title: Doupi (Stuffed Skin)
culturalTips.content: 豆皮 (doupi) is a savory pancake filled with sticky rice and meat. Head to Lao Tong Cheng or Hubu Lane for the best examples.

culturalTips.title: Crayfish Season
culturalTips.content: 夏天吃虾 (summer crayfish) is a Wuhan tradition. From May to September, crayfish restaurants pack out. 靓靓蒸虾 and 复地等都是热门选择.

culturalTips.title: Local Greetings
culturalTips.content: Wuhan people are known as direct. '吃了没' (have you eaten?) is a casual greeting. '劳慰' (thank you in Wuhan dialect) shows local flavor.

culturalTips.title: Yangtze River Safety
culturalTips.content: The Yangtze River is wide and dangerous. Swimming is prohibited in most areas. The river beaches (江滩) are for walking and viewing only.

culturalTips.title: Cherry Blossom Etiquette
culturalTips.content: During Wuhan University cherry blossom season (typically late March), visitors should stay on paths, not touch trees, and avoid weekends if possible.

culturalTips.title: Teahouse Culture
culturalTips.content: Wuhan has a traditional teahouse culture. 老通城等老字号茶馆体验武汉的茶文化.

culturalTips.title: Shopping Districts
culturalTips.content: 江汉路 and 光谷 are major shopping areas. 汉街 offers premium brands. 户部巷 is for souvenirs and local snacks.

culturalTips.title: Hankou History
culturalTips.content: The Hankou district has a rich international history as a treaty port. 黎黄陂路 and 武汉天地 showcase this colonial heritage.

culturalTips.title: Number Taboos
culturalTips.content: The number 4 is considered unlucky. The number 8 is very lucky and often used in business. Phone numbers with multiple 8s can be valuable.

culturalTips.title: Chopstick Etiquette
culturalTips.content: Never stick chopsticks vertically in rice or food. This resembles funeral incense and is considered very unlucky.

culturalTips.title: Metro Etiquette
culturalTips.content: Give seats to elderly, pregnant women, and people with children. Don't eat or drink on metro. Keep phone calls brief.

culturalTips.title: Chu Culture
culturalTips.content: Wuhan was the capital of the ancient Chu state. Hubei Provincial Museum showcases this rich cultural heritage with the famous Sword of King Goujian.

culturalTips.title: Summer Heat
culturalTips.content: Wuhan summers are extremely hot and humid (38C+). Stay hydrated, use sun protection, and take breaks in air-conditioned spaces.

culturalTips.title: Winter Cold
culturalTips.content: Wuhan winters are damp and cold (0-10C) with no central heating in most buildings. Dress in layers and expect indoor temperatures similar to outdoors.

culturalTips.title: River City Culture
culturalTips.content: Wuhan is known as the 'River City' for its unique location at the confluence of the Yangtze and Han Rivers. The rivers have shaped Wuhan's culture, cuisine, and identity for centuries.

culturalTips.title: Wuhan Breakfast (过早)
culturalTips.content: 过早 (guozao) is Wuhan's famous breakfast culture. The saying '早餐要吃好' (breakfast should be good) is taken seriously here. Try the breakfast street near Hubu Lane for authentic morning treats.

culturalTips.title: Spring Cherry Blossoms
culturalTips.content: Wuhan University's cherry blossom season (late March to early April) attracts visitors from across China. The 樱花大道 (Cherry Blossom Avenue) is particularly stunning. Weekdays are less crowded.

culturalTips.title: Wuhan Nightlife
culturalTips.content: Wuhan comes alive at night. 光谷 and 武汉天地 are popular nightlife districts. 黎黄陂路 has charming colonial-era cafes and bars. Summer nights are perfect for riverside walks.

culturalTips.title: Three Towns Culture
culturalTips.content: Wuhan consists of three historic towns: Wuchang (cultural), Hankou (commercial), and Hanyang (industrial). Each has its own character and specialties. Metro Line 2 connects all three.
